mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates broad economic expansion, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns as the market prices in a stronger fundamental outlook.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS is a key coincident indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in PAYEMS shows a negative rank correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation exceeds a critical threshold, it triggers expectations of monetary tightening by the central bank, increasing discount rates and negatively impacting equity valuations.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a tight labor market (low unemployment) and restrictive monetary policy (high Fed Funds rate) signals an overheating economy, increasing the risk of a policy-induced recession and causing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward Information Coefficient (IC) is positive over any 3-year rolling window, indicating the combination is actually bullish.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime, unexpectedly strong retail sales growth is interpreted negatively by markets as it increases the likelihood of further, more aggressive rate hikes to curb demand-pull inflation.
failure_condition: A positive surprise in RSAFS growth during tightening cycles is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in a statistically significant number of cases (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in industrial production indicates a contraction in the manufacturing and supply side of the economy, leading to lower earnings forecasts and negative equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a component of the Index of Coincident Economic Indicators.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns with an accuracy greater than a 50% baseline during NBER-dated recessions.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in housing starts signals strong consumer confidence and forward-looking investment, which translates into positive expectations for broader economic growth and corporate profits.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a major leading indicator of the business cycle.
failure_condition: The 6-month change in HOUST has a statistically significant negative correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: An accommodative monetary policy, reflected by a near-zero federal funds rate, reduces the cost of capital for firms and lowers the discount rate for future earnings, boosting equity prices over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS is 0.11, meeting the trigger condition.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery / Zero Interest Rate Policy (ZIRP)
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, the average 5-day forward return for the S&P 500 is negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where recent real GDP growth is strong but payroll growth is decelerating signals a potential peak in the business cycle, as firms slow hiring in anticipation of a future slowdown, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth > 0.5% AND 3-month PAYEMS growth < 0.1%
failure_condition: The specified divergence pattern is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observed instances over the last three business cycles.
